Totango formatting guidance

Keep objective titles short, descriptions operational, and milestones measurable. For plan summaries use Microsite and Resources, About the Dealership Group, Success Plan Goals, Key Hurts and Focus Areas.

Quality control checklist

Objectives tie to outcomes, milestones are measurable, updates avoid duplication, support summaries include expected versus actual, touchpoints include next steps and alignment.

CSM Workflow

Faster navigation, less scrolling, better command selection.
Favorites 0 saved

Recently used 0 recent
Build a prompt and the last commands you used will appear here.
How to use this tool
Just finished a dealer call

Start with Check In for the recap, then move to Objectives or Touchpoints if you need execution follow up.

Building or updating a plan

Use Success Plan first, then Objectives and Milestones to tighten ownership and timing.

Preparing for leadership review

Run QBR Prep or Internal Strategy first, then build the Executive Business Review output.

Working a live issue

Choose Support, Escalation Summary, or CSS Request depending on whether you need triage, executive clarity, or specialist help.

Account ownership is changing

Use Account Transition or Handoff to preserve context, open risks, and next best actions.

Seeing churn or adoption risk

Use Renewal or Retention Risk to frame exposure, blockers, mitigation steps, and executive messaging.

Workflow steps
1 Capture the signal

Pull transcript notes, support themes, health risks, and decisions before you write anything.

2 Pick the right command

Use search, favorites, or the guide above instead of hunting through the full library every time.

3 Build and export

Generate the prompt, send it to your approved AI tool, then bring the refined output back into Totango.

4 Operationalize

Update plans, objectives, touchpoints, support follow ups, and QBR narratives with clear ownership.

Shortcuts and onsite CSS request steps

Use Export to AI then paste into your company authorized AI tool. If copy fails, select text and press Ctrl C.

How to request an onsite CSS in Salesforce

1 Open Salesforce and navigate to the dealer account page.

2 Select the Activity tab and click Email. Expand the email window if necessary.

3 Click Insert Template and choose All Lightning Templates.

4 Select the template Customer Success Specialist Onsite Training Request Form.

5 Complete all fields including dealer group name, store location if applicable, training objectives, departments involved, number of users, and requested timeline.

6 Review the request and send the email. CSS typically responds within 24 business hours.

7 The request logs in Salesforce Activity History for the account.

What Good Looks Like

A green account is governance cadence, adoption milestones, executive visibility, clean documentation, and the right command used at the right time.
